pi_items.source: Verified across setup_jitsi.sh (line 36 creates /home/ga/.jitsi-meet-cfg/{web,...}), docker-compose.yml (line 10 mounts /home/ga/.jitsi-meet-cfg/web:/config:Z for the web service), setup_task.sh (line 17 explicitly sets CONFIG_PATH=/home/ga/.jitsi-meet-cfg/web/config.js), and export_result.sh (line 9 uses the same path). This is the standard Docker volume mount location for Jitsi Meet web configuration.

Verified across task.json description (line 5 specifies 'save it to /home/ga/bandwidth_test_evidence.png'), export_result.sh (line 10 checks this path and copies it to /tmp/evidence.png), and verifier.py (line 100 reads the evidence file from this path). This is the designated output path for the visual evidence screenshot.

docker-compose.yml (line 3 defines service name as 'web', not 'jitsi-web'); setup_task.sh (line 23 references 'jitsi_meet_env-web-1' when copying from container); export_result.sh (line 33 uses 'docker ps -qf "name=web"' for filtering). Standard docker-compose naming convention appends numeric suffix (-1) to service name. The metadata claims 'jitsi-web' which is incomplete; the service name is 'web' and the full container name would include a project prefix and numeric suffix.

Verified across task.json description (lines 5-6 specify 'video.height.ideal: 360' and 'video.height.max: 360'); Jitsi Meet official configuration documentation (https://raw.githubusercontent.com/jitsi/jitsi-meet/master/config.js) shows default constraints are 'ideal: 720, max: 720, min: 240' for HD video. The task configures low bandwidth profile by reducing resolution to 360p. The expected value 360 represents the target video height in pixels for the low bandwidth optimization.

privileged_info_summary: This is a synthetic task simulating a real-world system optimization scenario. The task configures Jitsi Meet (version stable-9753 in Docker) to enforce a low bandwidth profile. The config file is located at /home/ga/.jitsi-meet-cfg/web/config.js as a Docker volume mount from the jitsi/web container. The agent must modify two configuration parameters: set startAudioOnly to true (forces audio-only joining) and configure video constraints to use 360p resolution (ideal and max height of 360 pixels) instead of the default 720p, with a minimum height of 180 pixels. The container name should be 'web' (service name) or a full name like 'jitsi-meet-env-web-1' (depending on project prefix), not the claimed 'jitsi-web'. Evidence is saved to /home/ga/bandwidth_test_evidence.png as a screenshot showing the audio-only state after configuration. The Jitsi Meet version supports both the startAudioOnly option and W3C-compliant video constraints configuration.
